failed.title: ISSUE_001: Percentage (20%) is the controlling affordable housing standard
failed.match: PASS if the draft establishes the 20% percentage as the controlling standard for affordable housing (not a fixed unit count), so that if unit counts change during design development the affordable obligation adjusts proportionally. FAIL if the draft uses only a fixed unit count (e.g., 100 or 106 units) without tying it to the 20% percentage.

failed.title: ISSUE_001: Recalculation mechanism if unit counts change
failed.match: PASS if the draft includes a mechanism or provision for recalculating the number of required affordable units if total residential unit counts change during design development or phase rescoping. FAIL if there is no such recalculation mechanism.

failed.title: ISSUE_007: Includes performance security provisions for later phases
failed.match: PASS if the draft addresses performance security for subsequent phases — whether by requiring bonds/LCs for each phase, setting amounts/formulas, providing release mechanisms upon phase completion, or allowing the Phase I security to be replaced/supplemented. FAIL if the draft addresses Phase I security but is entirely silent on Phases II–IV.

failed.title: ISSUE_008: Incorporates $17.50/hour living wage for permanent retail/hotel employees
failed.match: PASS if the draft incorporates the $17.50/hour minimum living wage for permanent retail and hotel employees from Resolution condition #3(b). FAIL if this component is missing from the draft.

failed.title: ISSUE_009: Identifies LEED certification timing vs. Substantial Completion
failed.match: PASS if the draft identifies the issue that LEED Gold certification (Resolution condition #6) typically occurs months after building completion due to post-construction verification, and that tying LEED certification to Substantial Completion could disrupt the phasing timeline (since the next phase's commencement deadline runs from Substantial Completion). FAIL if this timing issue is not identified.

failed.title: ISSUE_009: Separate LEED certification deadline post-completion
failed.match: PASS if the draft establishes a separate deadline for achieving LEED Gold certification after Substantial Completion (e.g., 12 months post-Substantial Completion or similar reasonable period). FAIL if there is no separate LEED certification deadline.

failed.title: ISSUE_009: Consequences for failure to achieve LEED Gold
failed.match: PASS if the draft specifies consequences for failure to achieve LEED Gold certification (e.g., cure periods, acceptance of alternative certifications/equivalents, financial penalties, or liquidated damages). FAIL if no consequences are specified.

failed.title: ISSUE_011: Identifies force majeure 18-month cap ambiguity
failed.match: PASS if the draft identifies the ambiguity in whether the 18-month aggregate force majeure cap applies per-phase or project-wide, and notes that a project-wide cap may be insufficient for a multi-phase project spanning 8-10 years. FAIL if this ambiguity is not identified.

failed.title: ISSUE_011: Clarifies force majeure cap application (per-phase)
failed.match: PASS if the draft clarifies that the force majeure extension cap applies on a per-phase basis (or otherwise addresses the cascading delay risk across phases), rather than leaving an ambiguous project-wide aggregate cap. FAIL if the draft simply recites '18-month aggregate' without clarifying per-phase vs. project-wide application.

failed.title: Includes equity demonstration requirement ($85M within 60 days)
failed.match: PASS if the draft includes a requirement that Developer demonstrate committed equity of at least $85,000,000 within 60 days of the resolution (i.e., by approximately April 19, 2025), consistent with Resolution condition #1. FAIL if this requirement is omitted or the amount/timeline is incorrect.

deliverable:
# Development Agreement Draft — Summary of Work

## Deliverable
`output/development-agreement-draft.docx` — a draft Development Agreement for the **Lakemont Station Commons** mixed-use transit-oriented development, prepared on behalf of **Granite City Development LLC** (the "Developer") for negotiation with the **City of Lakemont, Illinois**.

The document is ~17,000 words, organized into 18 Articles plus Recitals, a signature page, and a consolidated schedule. It was generated from markdown via Pandoc and validated against the ECMA-376 WordprocessingML schema (validation passed).

## Source documents reviewed (all read in full)
1. **Negotiated Term Sheet** (Jan. 22, 2025) — principal business terms (non-binding).
2. **RFP Response Narrative** (Dec. 16, 2024) — Developer's proposal.
3. **City Council Resolution No. R-2025-017** (Feb. 18, 2025; vote 7–2) — conditional approval with 8 conditions.
4. **Construction Loan Term Sheet** (First Prairie National Bank; March 2025) — $210M facility, lender requirements re: the Development Agreement.
5. **Lakeshore Side Letter** (Feb. 10, 2025) — 25% equity investor's consent, anti-dilution, 14% Minimum IRR, 51% change-of-control threshold.
6. **Phase II ESA Summary** (Greenfield Environmental; Nov. 8, 2024) — environmental findings (BTEX + TCE on Parcel 016; metals on Parcel 017).
7. **TIF District Summary Memo** (Corporation Counsel; Mar. 5, 2025) — TIF parameters, eligible costs, increment projections, prevailing-wage analysis.

## Approach
- Drafted developer-favorable terms throughout (e.g., no reversionary interest; broad City environmental indemnity; limited/narrowed prevailing-wage scope; good-faith LEED pursuit; flexible permitted-assignment regime; robust Developer termination rights; lender-protective provisions).
- Embedded **54 bracketed drafting notes** inline, each flagging a cross-document conflict or open item with citations to the specific source documents and sections.
- Appended a **consolidated Schedule of Cross-Document Conflicts and Open Items** (54-row table) keyed to Agreement sections for ease of negotiation reference.

## Principal conflicts flagged (highest priority)
1. **Reversionary interest vs. lender prohibition (CRITICAL).** Resolution R-2025-017, Condition 8 requires a reversionary interest if Phase I is not Substantially Complete within 36 months of conveyance; the Construction Loan Term Sheet (§§4.2, 7.1, 12) states this "would make the Loan impossible to close," and the Lakeshore Side Letter (§7(d)) requires Investor consent. The draft eliminates the reversionary interest and substitutes alternative clawback mechanisms (§9.3).
2. **36-month reversion trigger vs. Sept. 30, 2028 Phase I SC deadline.** The reversion could fire ~3 months before the contractual completion deadline (§3.3 note).
3. **Prevailing-wage scope.** Term Sheet says "all construction"; Developer position limits it to statutory public-works scope (§6.3 note).
4. **Living wage / first-source hiring for permanent positions.** Required by Resolution Condition 3(c) but absent from the Term Sheet (§8.4 note).
5. **Change-of-control thresholds.** 50% (Term Sheet), [50]% (Loan), 51% (Side Letter) — must be harmonized (§11.4 note).
6. **TIF cap reachability.** TIF Memo projects only $38–42M (adjusted for abatement) vs. the $48M cap; Developer bears shortfall risk (§4.2 note).
7. **Land write-down / infrastructure funding source.** Whether charged against the $48M TIF cap (§§4.1, 4.3 notes).
8. **Equity demonstration deadline.** April 19, 2025 (Resolution) vs. 60 days after Agreement approval (Term Sheet) (§13.2 note).
9. **TCE contamination** on Parcel 016 not described in the Term Sheet (BTEX only) (§5.2 note).
10. **Insurance limits** — CGL aggregate $20M (Term Sheet) vs. $25M (Loan), plus additional coverages (§6.5 note).

The full set of 54 items is captured in the consolidated schedule at the end of the document.

You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Approach to the work

Before drafting, work the matter methodically. These steps apply to any
legal deliverable; adapt the emphasis to the task.

1. **Read every document fully, then fix the scope.** `read` each file in
   `$DOCUMENTS_DIR` completely — decisive facts hide in late schedules,
   exhibits, defined-term sections, and worked tables, not just the
   narrative. Then determine, from the task's framing plus the operative
   transactional/instruction documents, **exactly

**NEGOTIATED TERM SHEET**

**LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS** **MIXED-USE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT**

January 22, 2025

**Between:**

**Granite City Development LLC**, an Illinois limited liability company (\"Developer\")

**and**

**City of Lakemont, Illinois**, an Illinois home rule municipality (\"City\")

This Term Sheet summarizes the principal business terms negotiated between Developer and City for the development of the Lakemont Station Commons project (the \"Project\"). This Term Sheet is non-binding and is intended solely to facilitate the preparation of a definitive Development Agreement (the \"Agreement\"). Neither party shall have any binding obligation unless and until a definitive Agreement is executed and delivered by both parties and approved by the Lakemont City Council.

Developer was selected by the City through a competitive Request for Proposals process (RFP No. 2024-CD-018) conducted by the City\'s Community Development Department during 2024. Developer\'s proposal was evaluated and recommended for selection by the evaluation committee and thereafter by the City Manager. This Term Sheet reflects the principal terms negotiated between the parties following Developer\'s selection, subject to anticipated City Council approval. The terms set forth herein are further subject to the adoption by the City Council of a resolution authorizing the negotiation and execution of the definitive Agreement, which the parties anticipate will be considered at the City Council meeting scheduled for February 18, 2025.

**[Section 1: Parties]{.underline}**

**1.1 Developer**

> • **Entity Name:** Granite City Development LLC
>
> • **Jurisdiction:** Illinois limited liability company, formed March 14, 2019
>
> • **Principal Office:** 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200, Chicago, IL 60607
>

  Pellegrini Family Trust                  60%

  Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP   25%

  TransitWorks Equity Group LLC            15%
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Marcus J. Pellegrini serves as Trustee of the Pellegrini Family Trust and as Manager and CEO of the Developer entity. TransitWorks Equity Group LLC is a transit-oriented development investment vehicle managed by former public transit officials and institutional investors.

**1.3 City**

> • **Entity Name:** City of Lakemont, Illinois
>

>

>
> • **Corporation Counsel:** Rebecca A. Sinclair, Esq.

The as-is value reflects the environmental conditions described below.
>
> • **Environmental Status:** A Phase II Environmental Site Assessment was completed by Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC (report dated November 8, 2024). The Phase II ESA identified petroleum hydrocarbon contamination (BTEX compounds) in soil and groundwater on Parcel 12-24-300-016, affecting approximately 1.8 acres. Low-level metals contamination was identified in shallow soil on Parcel 12-24-300-017. Remediation is required for both parcels. Further details regarding environmental conditions and remediation are set forth in Section 6 below.

**[RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS]{.underline}**

**[Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented Development]{.underline}**

**[Lakemont Central Metra Station Site]{.underline}**

 

**Project Name:**

**[LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS]{.underline}**

 

**Submitted by:**

**GRANITE CITY DEVELOPMENT LLC** 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200 Chicago, Illinois 60607

 

**Submitted to:**

**City of Lakemont, Illinois** Community Development Department

Attention: Paul R. Vasquez, Director

200 Civic Center Drive Lakemont, Illinois 60048

 

**Date of Submission:** December 16, 2024

 

**Primary Contact:** Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager/CEO Telephone: (312) 555-4820

Email: mpellegrini@granitecitydev.com

**Legal Counsel:** Thornfield & Pratt LLP Catherine M. Albrecht, Partner 311 South Wacker Drive, Suite 5400 Chicago, Illinois 60606 Telephone: (312) 555-9100

Email: calbrecht@thornfieldpratt.com

 

PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL --- Submitted in Response to City of Lakemont RFP No. 2024-CD-07. This document contains trade secrets, proprietary business information, and confidential financial data of Granite City Development LLC and its investors. Unauthorized reproduction or distribution is prohibited.

**[Section 1: Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Granite City Development LLC (\"Developer\"), an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2019, is pleased to submit this proposal in response to the City of Lakemont\'s Request for Proposals No. 2024-CD-07 for the development of the approximately 12.7-acre site located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048, adjacent to the new Lakemont Central Metra station. The Developer proposes to create **Lakemont Station Commons**, a transformative mixed-use transit-oriented development that will serve as a catalyst for economic growth, community vitality, and sustainable urban design in the City of Lakemont.

The site consists of three contiguous parcels --- PIN 12-24-300-015, PIN 12-24-300-016, and PIN 12-24-300-017 --- totaling approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet), to be consolidated prior to development. Lakemont Station Commons will deliver approximately **1,620,000 gross square feet** of mixed-use development across four carefully sequenced phases, with an estimated total hard construction cost of **\$340,000,000** and a total project cost (inclusive of soft costs, land acquisition, financing costs, and environmental remediation) of approximately **\$412,000,000**.

The project program comprises:

> • **530 residential units**, including 106 affordable units priced at or below 60% of Area Median Income, representing 20% of all residential units;
>
> • **280,000 square feet** of Class A office space;
>
> • **63,000 square feet** of ground-floor retail;
>
> • A **150-room select-service hotel**;
>
> • A **2.5-acre public plaza** designed as the project\'s civic centerpiece;
>
> • A **28,000 square-foot community center** to be conveyed to the City upon completion; and
>
> • **1,430 structured parking spaces** across three parking facilities.

The four-phase delivery plan spans approximately eight to ten years, with Phase I groundbreaking targeted no later than **March 1, 2026**. The Developer is committed to achieving **LEED Gold certification** for all buildings within the development, reinforcing Lakemont Station Commons\' role as a model for sustainable transit-oriented design.

The project will deliver robust community benefits, including a local hiring commitment of 35% Lakemont residents for construction employment, affordable housing exceeding minimum requirements, and significant public amenities. The remediation of a former industrial brownfield site will eliminate legacy environmental contamination, converting a blighted property into a vibrant, productive community asset.

The Developer is led by **Marcus J. Pellegrini**, age 54, who brings 28 years of experience in Chicagoland commercial real estate development. Mr. Pellegrini has successfully developed notable projects including The Halsted Collection (mixed-use, Chicago), Riverside Crossing (transit-oriented development, Naperville), and Archer Park Residences (affordable housing, Chicago). The Developer entity maintains a net worth of approximately **\$185 million**, audited by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP, and has bonding capacity of up to \$75 million through Ridgepoint Surety Corporation.

The Developer\'s ownership comprises the Pellegrini Family Trust (60%), Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP (25%), and TransitWorks Equity Group LLC (15%). The project is supported by **\$85 million in committed equity** --- \$51 million from Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP and \$34 million from the Pellegrini Family Trust --- together with a **\$210 million preliminary construction loan commitment** from First Prairie National Bank. This capital base, combined with anticipated public incentives including TIF reimbursement of up to \$48 million, positions the project for successful execution across all four phases.

**[Section 2: Developer Qualifications and Experience]{.underline}**

**[2.1 Firm Overview]{.underline}**

Granite City Development LLC was formed on March 14, 2019, as an Illinois limited liability company, with its principal office located at 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200, Chicago, Illinois 60607. Since its formation, the Developer has focused exclusively on complex, large-scale mixed-use and transit-oriented development projects throughout the greater Chicagoland metropolitan area.

The firm is led by **Marcus J. Pellegrini**, who serves as Manager and Chief Executive Officer. Mr. Pellegrini, age 54, brings 28 years of hands-on experience in commercial real estate development, spanning project conceptualization, entitlement, financing, construction management, and lease-up. His career has encompassed over \$600 million in completed development projects across a range of product types, including mixed-use u

*City of Lakemont, Illinois*

**A RESOLUTION GRANTING CONDITIONAL APPROVAL FOR THE LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS MIXED-USE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT PROJECT AT 1400--1550 LAKEMONT STATION BOULEVARD AND AUTHORIZING THE CITY MANAGER TO NEGOTIATE AND EXECUTE A DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT WITH GRANITE CITY DEVELOPMENT LLC, SUBJECT TO CONDITIONS**

**WHEREAS**, the City of Lakemont, Illinois, is a home rule municipality with a population of approximately 87,000, organized and existing under the Constitution and laws of the State of Illinois, including the broad powers of local self-governance granted under Article VII, Section 6 of the Illinois Constitution of 1970, and is authorized to exercise any power and perform any function pertaining to its government and affairs; and

**WHEREAS**, the City is the owner of certain real property commonly known as 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, IL 60048, consisting of three (3) separate parcels of record identified by the following Parcel Identification Numbers: 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017, totaling approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet) (collectively, the \"Property\"). The City acquired Parcels 12-24-300-015 and 12-24-300-016 through tax forfeiture proceedings concluded in 2016, and acquired Parcel 12-24-300-017 by direct purchase in 2018. The Property constitutes the former site of the Lakemont Ironworks manufacturing campus, which ceased industrial operations in 2009 and has remained substantially vacant and underutilized since that time. The Property is situated immediately adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra commuter rail station, providing direct access to commuter rail service on the Metra Milwaukee District/North Line; and

**WHEREAS**, the Property is currently zoned M-2 (General Manufacturing) under the City of Lakemont Zoning Ordinance, as amended. **WHEREAS**, the City, through its Community Development Department under the direction of Paul R. Vasquez, Director, conducted a competitive Request for Proposals (\"RFP\") process for the redevelopment of the Property as a mixed-use transit-oriented development, consistent with the City\'s objectives of leveraging the public investment in the Lakemont Central Metra station, stimulating economic development in the surrounding area, generating tax revenues, creating employment opportunities, and providing housing options at a range of affordability levels. The Developer has a reported entity-level net worth of approximately One Hundred Eighty-Five Million Dollars (\$185,000,000), with audited financial statements available through Clearmont Accounting Group LLP, and maintains bonding capacity of up to Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$75,000,000) through Ridgepoint Surety Corporation; and

**WHEREAS**, the Developer has proposed a four-phase mixed-use transit-oriented development to be known as \"Lakemont Station Commons\" (the \"Project\"), with a total gross building area of approximately 1,620,000 square feet, comprising the following program elements and estimated costs:

> *Phase I* --- Three hundred twenty (320) residential dwelling units, consisting of two hundred fifty-six (256) market-rate units and sixty-four (64) affordable housing units available to households earning at or below sixty percent (60%) of the Area Median Income (\"AMI\"); forty-five thousand (45,000) square feet of ground-floor retail space; and a four hundred eighty (480)-space structured parking facility. Phase I shall comprise approximately four hundred twenty thousand (420,000) square feet of gross building area, with an estimated hard construction cost of One Hundred Five Million Dollars (\$105,000,000).

This document is for discussion purposes only and does not constitute a commitment, agreement, or obligation to lend by First Prairie National Bank. All terms are subject to the conditions and qualifications set forth in Section 14 below.

**[1. PARTIES AND PROJECT SUMMARY]{.underline}**

**Lender:** First Prairie National Bank (\"First Prairie\" or \"Lender\"), a nationally chartered banking institution with main office at 135 South LaSalle Street, Chicago, IL 60603.

Guarantors to provide a personal/trust guaranty of completion and a partial repayment guaranty under a burn-down structure, the specific terms of which are to be determined and documented in the definitive loan agreements.

**Project:** Lakemont Station Commons (the \"Project\") --- a mixed-use transit-oriented development on approximately 12.7 acres (Parcel IDs: 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, 12-24-300-017, to be consolidated) adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra station, consisting of approximately 1,620,000 gross square feet across four phases, including:

> • Residential: 530 units
>
> • Office: 280,000 sq ft
>
> • Retail: 63,000 sq ft
>
> • Hotel: 150 rooms
>
> • Community Center: 28,000 sq ft
>
> • Public Plaza: 2.5 acres
>
> • Structured Parking: 1,430 spaces

**Total Estimated Project Cost:** Approximately \$412,000,000.

**Total Hard Construction Cost:** Approximately \$340,000,000.

**[2. LOAN TERMS]{.underline}**

**2.1 Facility Amount and Structure**

**Facility Amount:** Up to \$210,000,000 (the \"Loan\" or \"Construction Facility\").

**Structure:** Revolving construction loan facility with phased draw-down. Loan proceeds available for Phase I and Phase II hard and soft construction costs. Availability for Phases III and IV subject to a separate supplemental commitment or amendment, to be negotiated at the appropriate time.

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Component**                      **Sub-Limit**           **Description**
  ---------------------------------- ----------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Phase I**                        \$105,000,000           Residential Tower A + Retail Podium: 320 residential units, 45,000 sq ft retail, 480 parking spaces (\~420,000 gross sq ft)

  **Phase II**                       \$76,000,000            Office Building + Retail: 280,000 sq ft office, 18,000 sq ft retail, 350 parking spaces (\~380,000 gross sq ft)

  **Infrastructure / Contingency**   \$29,000,000            Infrastructure costs, contingencies, and pre-development costs, subject to Lender-approved budget
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Soft Cost Allocation:** Up to 20% of each phase sub-limit may be allocated to eligible soft costs (architecture, engineering, permitting, legal, accounting, pre-opening costs) --- approximately \$21,000,000 for Phase I and \$15,200,000 for Phase II.

**2.2 Interest Rate and Fees**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**                            **Terms**
  ----------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Interest Rate**                   SOFR (30-day term) + 275 basis points; SOFR floor of 1.00%

  **Default Rate**                    Applicable rate + 400 basis points

  **Commitment Fee**                  0.50% of total Facility Amount (\$1,050,000), payable at closing

  **Unused Line Fee**                 0.25% per annum on daily average undrawn balance, payable quarterly

  **Extension Fee**                   0.25% of then-outstanding balance per extension period

  **Exit Fee**                        None
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Interest shall be payable monthly, in arrears. An interest reserve shall be funded from Loan proceeds, estimated at a 24-month interest reserve for Phase I.

**2.3 Term and Maturity**

**Initial Term:** 36 months from initial closing/first draw.

**Extension Options:** Two (2) six-month extensions, each subject to: (a) no existing Event of Default; (b) payment of the extension fee; (c) achievement of minimum construction milestones to be agreed upon in definitive documentation; and (d) an updated appraisal demonstrating compliance with the minimum loan-to-value ratio.

**Maximum Term:** 48 months (36 + 6 + 6).

**Phase I Construction Period:** Estimated at 30 months. Groundbreaking shall occur no later than March 1, 2026, with Substantial Completion targeted by September 30, 2028.

**[PHASE II ENVIRONMENTAL SITE ASSESSMENT]{.underline}**

**[EXECUTIVE SUMMARY REPORT]{.underline}**

**LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS**

**1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard** **Lakemont, Illinois 60048**

 

**Prepared for:**

**Granite City Development LLC** 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200 Chicago, Illinois 60607

Attention: Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager/CEO

 

**Prepared by:**

**Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC** 450 North Michigan Avenue, Suite 1800 Chicago, Illinois 60611

 

**Report Date:** November 8, 2024

**GEC Project Number:** GEC-2024-0847

 

**CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:** This report was prepared for the exclusive use of Granite City Development LLC and its authorized agents, including its legal counsel Thornfield & Pratt LLP. Distribution to third parties, including the City of Lakemont, Illinois, and any prospective lender (including First Prairie National Bank), is authorized only with prior written consent of Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC or as required by applicable regulatory authorities. Any reliance by third parties on the findings or conclusions herein requires execution of a reliance letter from Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC. Unauthorized reproduction, distribution, or use of this report, in whole or in part, is strictly prohibited.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[1. Introduction and Purpose]{.underline}**

**[1.1 Authorization and Scope]{.underline}**

Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC (\"GEC\") was retained by Granite City Development LLC (\"Developer\") to conduct a Phase II Environmental Site Assessment (\"Phase II ESA\") of three parcels comprising approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet) located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048 (collectively, the \"Site\"). The assessment was authorized by Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager/CEO of Granite City Development LLC, pursuant to an engagement letter dated August 12, 2024.

The Phase II ESA was conducted to evaluate recognized environmental conditions (\"RECs\") identified in the Phase I Environmental Site Assessment previously prepared by GEC (GEC Project No. GEC-2024-0612, dated July 19, 2024) (the \"Phase I ESA\") and to characterize soil and groundwater quality across all three parcels comprising the Site. The Phase I ESA identified several RECs associated with historical industrial operations on the property, including petroleum storage, metal finishing operations involving chlorinated solvents, and the placement of fill material of uncertain provenance on portions of the Site. The present Phase II ESA was designed to investigate these RECs through a comprehensive subsurface sampling program, including the advancement of soil borings, installation of temporary groundwater monitoring wells, and laboratory analysis of soil and groundwater samples for a range of contaminants of concern.

The investigation is intended to support the Developer\'s due diligence in connection with the potential acquisition of the Site from the City of Lakemont, Illinois, for development of a mixed-use transit-oriented project adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra station (the \"Lakemont Station Commons\" project). The Site is currently zoned M-2 (General Manufacturing) under the City of Lakemont Zoning Ordinance. The Developer has submitted an application for rezoning of the Site to PD-TOD (Planned Development--Transit Oriented District) to accommodate the proposed mixed-use development. The City of Lakemont acquired the three parcels comprising the Site through two distinct transactions: Parcel 12-24-300-015 was acquired by the City through tax forfeiture proceedings in 2016, and Parcels 12-24-300-016 and 12-24-300-017 were acquired by the City through direct purchase in 2018. The Developer intends to acquire the consolidated Site from the City pursuant to a development agreement currently under negotiation.

**[1.2 Regulatory Framework]{.underline}**

The Phase II ESA was conducted in general conformance with ASTM Standard E1903-19, *Standard Practice for Environmental Site Assessments: Phase II Environmental Site Assessment Process*, and applicable Illinois Environmental Protection Agency (\"IEPA\") guidance documents, including the IEPA Voluntary Site Remediation Program (\"VSRP\") Guidance Document (Publication 2024) and the IEPA Technical Guidance for Investigation and Remediation of Contaminated Sites. The subsurface investigation was designed to collect data sufficient to evaluate site conditions against the remediation objectives established under the IEPA Tiered Approach to Corrective Action Objectives (\"TACO\"), codified at 35 Illinois Administrative Code Part 742.

The TACO framework establishes three tiers of remediation objectives for soil and groundwater. Tier 1 objectives are generic, risk-based standards applicable statewide. Tier 2 objectives are site-specific standards developed using modified exposure parameters or institutional and engineering controls. Tier 3 objectives are derived from a site-specific risk assessment. The analytical results from this investigation have been compared to Tier 1 remediation objectives for both residential and industrial/commercial land use scenarios, as well as to Class I groundwater quality standards established under 35 Illinois Administrative Code Part 620.

The findings of this Phase II ESA will be used to determine whether enrollment in the IEPA VSRP is warranted for any or all of the three parcels and whether a No Further Remediation (\"NFR\") letter can be obtained for the Site or individual parcels upon completion of any necessary remedial activities. The VSRP provides a voluntary, incentive-based mechanism for investigation and remediation of contaminated properties under IEPA oversight, culminating in the issuance of an NFR letter upon satisfactory completion of approved remedial actions.

**[1.3 Parcels Investigated]{.underline}**

The Site consists of three separa

**PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATION / ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT**

**[MEMORANDUM]{.underline}**

**TO:** Gerald T. Fong, City Manager, City of Lakemont, Illinois

**FROM:** Rebecca A. Sinclair, Esq., Corporation Counsel, City of Lakemont

**DATE:** March 5, 2025

**RE:** Lakemont Central TIF District --- Summary of Parameters, Eligible Costs, Increment Projections, and Prevailing Wage Applicability in Connection with Proposed Lakemont Station Commons Development

**CC:** Paul R. Vasquez, Director, Community Development Department; Jonathan Ashford, Ashford, Keene & Mulvaney LLP (outside municipal counsel)

**[I. Purpose and Background]{.underline}**

This memorandum has been prepared at the request of City Manager Gerald T. Fong in connection with the City of Lakemont\'s ongoing negotiation of a development agreement with Granite City Development LLC (\"Developer\") for the proposed Lakemont Station Commons project. On February 18, 2025, the City Council adopted Resolution R-2025-017, conditionally approving the selection of the Developer through a competitive Request for Proposals process. The City Manager and Marcus J. Pellegrini, on behalf of the Developer, executed a negotiated term sheet on January 22, 2025, outlining the preliminary parameters of the proposed public-private partnership.

The purpose of this memorandum is fourfold: (1) to provide a comprehensive summary of the Lakemont Central TIF District\'s legal parameters and remaining duration; (2) to identify eligible reimbursable project costs under the Illinois Tax Increment Allocation Redevelopment Act, 65 ILCS 5/11-74.4-3 (the \"TIF Act\"); (3) to project the tax increment available to support the \$48 million TIF reimbursement cap negotiated in the term sheet; and (4) to analyze prevailing wage obligations triggered by the various forms of public financial assistance associated with the project.

The Lakemont Station Commons project is a mixed-use, transit-oriented development on approximately 12.7 acres (Parcels 12-24-300-015, -016, and -017) located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048, immediately adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra station. The project carries a total estimated cost of approximately \$412 million, with total hard construction costs of approximately \$340 million to be delivered across four phases. The project will include residential towers, office space, retail, a hotel, structured parking, a public plaza, and a community center.

This memorandum is intended to inform the development agreement negotiations and should be reviewed in conjunction with the Phase II Environmental Site Assessment Summary prepared by Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC (dated November 8, 2024) and the appraisal prepared by Crossland Appraisal Services (dated January 15, 2025, appraised value of \$18.4 million for the three subject parcels).

**[II. TIF District Establishment and Legal Framework]{.underline}**

**A. Establishing Ordinance and Statutory Authority**

The Lakemont Central TIF District was established by Ordinance No. 2019-42, adopted by the City Council on September 17, 2019, pursuant to the Illinois Tax Increment Allocation Redevelopment Act, 65 ILCS 5/11-74.4-1 *et seq.* The City of Lakemont is a home rule municipality with a population of approximately 87,000, exercising home rule powers under Article VII, Section 6 of the Illinois Constitution of 1970, in addition to the statutory TIF authority conferred by the TIF Act. The TIF district was designated on the basis of findings that the redevelopment project area qualified as a conservation area under the eligibility criteria then applicable under the TIF Act, including findings relating to obsolescence, deterioration, inadequate utilities, and environmental contamination. The Lakemont Central TIF District encompasses the project site and a number of surrounding parcels, including the area immediately adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra commuter rail station.

**B. District Boundaries and Included Parcels**

The three subject parcels --- 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017 --- are located within the Lakemont Central TIF District boundaries. These parcels are currently owned by the City, having been acquired through tax forfeiture proceedings in 2016 (Parcels -015 and -016) and by direct purchase in 2018 (Parcel -017). The parcels are presently zoned M-2 (General Manufacturing) and will require rezoning to PD-TOD (Planned Development--Transit Oriented District) as required by Condition No. 5 of Resolution R-2025-017. The three parcels have a combined acreage of approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet) and constitute the former Lakemont Ironworks manufacturing campus, which ceased operations in 2009. The parcels are to be consolidated into a single legal parcel prior to or concurrent with the land conveyance to the Developer under the forthcoming development agreement.

**C. TIF District Duration and Expiration**

**The Lakemont Central TIF District expires on December 31, 2042.** Under the TIF Act, the maximum statutory life of a TIF district is twenty-three (23) years from the date of the ordinance designating the redevelopment project area. Ordinance No. 2019-42 was adopted in 2019, and the 23-year statutory limit extends through 2042, consistent with the December 31, 2042 expiration date set forth in the establishing ordinance.

Upon expiration, all tax increment ceases to be allocated to the Special Tax Allocation Fund, and all overlapping taxing districts --- including Lakemont Community Unit School District 204, Lake County, and the Lakemont Park District --- resume receiving their full share of property tax revenue from parcels within the former TIF district.

This expiration date is a fixed statutory constraint that cannot be extended unilaterally by the City. An extension would require either amending state legislation to authorize a longer duration for this specific TI

turns.result:
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

# DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT

## LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS --- MIXED-USE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT

**DRAFT FOR NEGOTIATION --- PREPARED ON BEHALF OF THE DEVELOPER**

**Privileged and Confidential --- Attorney Work Product / Attorney-Client Communication**

> *This draft Development Agreement is prepared by Thornfield & Pratt LLP on behalf of Granite City Development LLC ("Developer") for negotiation with the City of Lakemont, Illinois ("City"). It reflects the principal business terms set forth in the Negotiated Term Sheet dated January 22, 2025, the conditional approval granted by City Council Resolution No. R-2025-017 (adopted February 18, 2025), and the Developer's proposal submitted in response to the City's Request for Proposals. Bracketed drafting notes throughout flag cross-document conflicts among the source materials and open items requiring resolution. Bracketed text in the body denotes terms to be confirmed or negotiated. This document is non-binding until executed by both parties and approved by the Lakemont City Council.*

**This DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT** (this "Agreement") is made and entered into as of \[●\], 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between **GRANITE CITY DEVELOPMENT LLC**, an Illinois limited liability company ("Developer"), and the **CITY OF LAKEMONT, ILLINOIS**, an Illinois home rule municipality ("City"). Developer and City are each referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

## RECITALS

**A.** The City is the owner of certain real property located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048, consisting of three parcels identified by Parcel Identification Numbers 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017, totaling approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet) (the "Site"), formerly occupied by the Lakemont Ironworks manufacturing campus and situated immediately adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra commuter rail station.

**B.** The City, through its Community Development Department, conducted a competitive Request for Proposals process for the redevelopment of the Site as a mixed-use transit-oriented development.

> **\[Drafting Note --- Cross-Document Conflict:\*\* The RFP Response Narrative (dated December 16, 2024) identifies the solicitation as RFP No. 2024-CD-07, while the Negotiated Term Sheet (dated January 22, 2025) identifies it as RFP No. 2024-CD-018. City Council Resolution No. R-2025-017 does not state a number. The correct RFP number must be confirmed and stated consistently in the Recitals and the defined term "RFP." \*\*\]**

**C.** Developer submitted a proposal in response to the RFP and was selected as the preferred developer based upon its qualifications, experience, project proposal, and demonstrated financial capacity.

**D.** Developer and the City executed a non-binding Negotiated Term Sheet dated January 22, 2025 (the "Term Sheet"), setting forth the principal business terms for the development of the Project.

**E.** On February 18, 2025, the Lakemont City Council adopted Resolution No. R-2025-017 (the "Resolution"), which granted conditional approval for the Project and authorized the City Manager to negotiate and execute this Agreement, subject to the conditions set forth in the Resolution.

> **\[Drafting Note --- Open Item:\*\* The Resolution was adopted by a vote of 7--2 (not unanimous). The two dissenting votes are a political-risk factor that may affect rezoning proceedings, any further City Council action, and public support. Developer should consider whether the Agreement should lock in the terms approved by the Resolution to minimize re-litigation of approved terms. \*\*\]**
>
> **\[Drafting Note --- Open Item:\*\* The Term Sheet (Section 18.2(v)) lists "Approval of the definitive Agreement by the Lakemont City Council" as a condition precedent to execution, while the Resolution (Section 2 and Section 4) authorizes the City Manager to negotiate and execute the Agreement upon satisfaction of the Resolution's conditions and approval as to form by Corporation Counsel and outside counsel --- without expressly requiring a separate Council vote on the definitive Agreement. As used in this Agreement, capitalized terms have the meanings set forth below or as otherwise defined in the body of this Agreement. turns.text:
I have completed the task. Here is a summary of what I delivered.

## Deliverable
**`output/development-agreement-draft.docx`** — a draft Development Agreement for the **Lakemont Station Commons** mixed-use transit-oriented development, prepared on behalf of **Granite City Development LLC** (the Developer) for negotiation with the City of Lakemont.

The document (~17,000 words) is organized into 18 Articles plus Recitals, a signature page, and a consolidated schedule. It was generated via Pandoc and **validated against the ECMA-376 WordprocessingML schema (passed)**.

## How I approached it
I read all seven source documents in full — the Negotiated Term Sheet, RFP Response Narrative, City Council Resolution R-2025-017, Construction Loan Term Sheet, Lakeshore Side Letter, Phase II ESA Summary, and TIF District Summary Memo — then cross-referenced them section by section to build a complete census of conflicts and open items.

I drafted **developer-favorable terms** throughout (no reversionary interest; broad uncapped City environmental indemnity covering all pre-existing contaminants including TCE; prevailing-wage scope narrowed to statutory public works; good-faith LEED pursuit rather than a strict certification guarantee; flexible permitted-assignment regime with a Key Person alternative; robust Developer termination rights; lender-protective provisions), and embedded **54 bracketed drafting notes** inline, each citing the specific source documents and sections in conflict. A consolidated **54-row Schedule of Cross-Document Conflicts and Open Items** at the end keys every item to its Agreement section.

## Highest-priority conflicts flagged
- **Reversionary interest vs. lender prohibition (CRITICAL):** Resolution Condition 8 requires a reversionary interest; the Construction Lender states it "would make the Loan impossible to close," and the Investor requires consent. The draft substitutes alternative clawback mechanisms (§9.3).
- **36-month reversion trigger vs. the Sept. 30, 2028 Phase I completion deadline** — the reversion could fire ~3 months early.
- **Prevailing-wage scope** ("all construction" vs. statutory public-works scope).
- **Living wage / first-source hiring for permanent positions** — required by the Resolution but absent from the Term Sheet.
- **Change-of-control thresholds** — 50% (Term Sheet), [50]% (Loan), 51% (Side Letter) must be harmonized.
- **TIF cap reachability** — TIF Memo projects only $38–42M (adjusted) vs. the $48M cap, with Developer bearing shortfall risk.
- **Land write-down / infrastructure funding source** — whether c
